post.headline: Set clear status text for incoming messages
post.subtext: Recipients can edit the update a sender sees after submitting a message, so sources know what to expect while the disclosure is reviewed.
post.image_alt_text: Social graphic showing the Hush Line desktop settings page for Message Statuses, with the Message Statuses tab selected and editable status text for a waiting message. The graphic headline reads, "Set clear status text for incoming messages."

social.bluesky:
After someone sends a sensitive message, they should know what happens next.

Hush Line lets recipients set the status text a sender sees during review, without turning the disclosure flow into live chat.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
social.linkedin:
When someone sends a sensitive message, uncertainty can make the waiting period harder.

Hush Line lets recipients set the status text a sender sees after submitting. A newsroom, law office, nonprofit, or other intake team can explain what happens next without turning the disclosure flow into live chat.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.
social.mastodon:
When someone sends a sensitive message, the waiting period should be clear.

Hush Line lets recipients set the status text a sender sees after submitting, so a newsroom, law office, or intake team can explain what happens next during review.

Learn more at https://hushline.app.

post.rationale: Selected the message statuses screenshot because notifications and directory screens both repeated prior-month themes. This recipient-shared screen supports a distinct workflow: helping journalists, lawyers, nonprofits, and other trusted recipients set clear sender-facing updates during message review.
post.copy_brief: Write for recipients and staff using Hush Line day to day, not for platform admins.
post.title: Settings - Message Statuses (artvandelay)

summary: One daily recipient-focused post for 2026-04-28 using the message statuses settings screenshot.
